main_sections.title: Spices that Heal Us
main_sections.prose_summary: This section opens with two pre-reading activities: students recall three spices used at home and match pictures of ten spices (turmeric, fenugreek, cumin seeds, asafoetida, cinnamon, clove, ginger, black pepper, fennel seeds, cardamom) to their names in their own language and in English. The main text is a letter from Daadi (grandmother) to her grandchildren Vikram and Vaibhavi, who have recovered from illness using natural cures she had earlier shared. Daadi traces her knowledge to her own grandmother and proceeds to describe the healing benefits of common kitchen spices: turmeric (haldi) improves energy, digestion, and reduces body pain; fenugreek (methi) seeds soaked overnight manage blood sugar and body weight; cumin (jeera) aids digestion and cures sleeplessness; asafoetida (heeng) relieves gas and controls cough and cold; cinnamon (dalchini) and clove (laung) give relief from toothache; ginger (adrak) helps with cough, cold, and pain, and has been used in cooking for over 4000 years; black pepper (kali mirch) aids digestion and relieves body pain; fennel seeds (saunf) and carom seeds (ajwain) aid digestion after meals; cardamom (elaichi) helps with digestion, respiratory troubles, and bad breath. Daadi closes by advising the children to consult an elder before using any natural cure and to share these remedies with friends. The text emphasises intergenerational transmission of traditional knowledge. A listening task introduces Tulsi (Holy Basil) — found in north central India, called 'the Queen of Herbs', which decreases glucose and cholesterol levels, treats breathing and digestion disorders, has anti-bacterial properties used in hand sanitisers, and forms the base for mouth wash and toothpaste; Tulsi tea calms the mind and the plant protects from pollutants.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the given lines and answer the following questions. 'When I was a child, I learnt them from my grandmother. She used to find cures for most of the weather-related common illnesses in the kitchen.'

question_bank.stem: Where exactly in the kitchen did her grandmother find the cures?
Give one example of weather-related common illnesses.
What kind of relationship did the speaker have with her grandmother?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Fill in the blanks to complete the sentence. Daadi soaked methi seeds overnight and drank the water in the morning to manage _____________________ and _____________________.

question_bank.stem: Daadi soaked methi seeds overnight and drank the water in the morning to manage _____________________ and _____________________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Circle the spice that is not useful for body pain according to Daadi's letter.

question_bank.stem: Which spice is NOT useful for body pain according to Daadi's letter? 1. Turmeric  2. Fennel seeds  3. Ginger  4. Black pepper

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Why did Daadi ask Vikram and Vaibhavi to share the natural cures with their friends?

question_bank.stem: Why did Daadi ask Vikram and Vaibhavi to share the natural cures with their friends?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: What was Daadi's final advice to Vikram and Vaibhavi?

question_bank.stem: What was Daadi's final advice to Vikram and Vaibhavi?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: How do we know that natural cures are passed from one generation to another?

question_bank.stem: How do we know that natural cures are passed from one generation to another?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Why do you think we should know about the healing properties of spices?

question_bank.stem: Why do you think we should know about the healing properties of spices?

listening.transcript_text: The wonder herb: Tulsi is a shrub in the basil family. It is said that it was first found in north central India and now grows almost everywhere in India. It is known as 'the Queen of Herbs' and has many medicinal uses. Studies show that it helps to decrease glucose and cholesterol levels and improve blood pressure. It is commonly used to treat breathing and digestion disorders. As it has anti-bacterial properties, it is used in herbal hand sanitisers. It is used as a base for making herbal mouth wash and toothpaste for treating bad breath, gum disease and mouth ulcers. Tulsi leaves can be washed and eaten raw. We can also add Tulsi leaves when we make tea. Tulsi tea helps in dealing with stress as it calms our mind. Tulsi protects us from a vast range of pollutants around us.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to the benefits of Tulsi (Holy Basil), a common herb. As you listen, complete the fact sheet given below.

question_bank.stem: Complete THE WONDER HERB fact sheet as you listen: (1) Name: Tulsi | Family: _______ (2) First found in: _______ (3) Now found: everywhere in India (4) Known as: The _______ of herbs (5) Can decrease levels of: _______ and cholesterol (6) Used to treat disorders of: breathing and _______ (7) Is used to make: mouth wash and _______ (8) We can have Tulsi leaves by: eating them _______ or adding them while making _______

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Daadi told Vikram and Vaibhavi about many spices. Choose one spice and introduce yourself as that spice to the class. Before you speak, make a spicy headband for the spice you chose (cut strips of paper 2 inches wide, join two ends to make a band, make a small card with a picture of the spice and colour it, attach the card on your headband). While speaking, mention: your name, part of the plant, colour, texture, taste, benefits.

Vikram's grandmother shared two natural cures to cure his cough in her earlier letter. Some of the words got washed away by the rain. Help Vikram complete the natural cures using the phrases from the box (two phrases not needed): some leaves of basil for cough, amount of honey, the mixture for high fever, a piece of fresh ginger, you can have it, grind to get, home remedy.

question_bank.stem: Complete natural cure 1 (ginger and honey cure for cough) by filling blanks with the correct phrases from the box.
Complete natural cure 2 (long pepper, dried ginger, basil, cardamom powder with honey) by filling blanks with the correct phrases from the box.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Now, write a short paragraph on Tulsi based on the completed fact sheet in 'Let us listen'.

question_bank.stem: Write a short paragraph on Tulsi using the information from the fact sheet you completed.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You have read the uses of spices in the letter. Complete the following table with the help of your teacher. Part of the plant: root/bark/leaves/flower buds/fruit/seeds. Texture: rough/soft/silky. Taste: bitter/sweet/sour/salty/spicy. Spices: Turmeric, Fenugreek, Cinnamon, Clove, Black pepper, Cardamom.

question_bank.stem: Complete the table for each spice — Part of plant, Colour, Texture, Taste. Spices: Turmeric, Fenugreek, Cinnamon, Clove, Black pepper, Cardamom.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the sentences from the letter (may, should, can, must, need to, used to are underlined). These are called modal verbs or modals. Each modal verb has a different function. (1) Match the helping verbs in Column A with their functions in Column B: (i) may — (ii) should — (iii) can — (iv) must — (v) need to — (vi) used to. Functions: A. past habit, B. suggestion, C. compulsion, D. advice, E. ability, F. necessity. (2) Complete the following dialogues with any four modals from the table above (use a modal only once).

question_bank.stem: Match modal verbs to their functions. Column A: (i) may, (ii) should, (iii) can, (iv) must, (v) need to, (vi) used to. Column B: A. past habit, B. suggestion, C. compulsion, D. advice, E. ability, F. necessity.

Complete the dialogue using any four modals (each used only once). Ajay: Anand had fever last week. He (i) _____________ take good rest. Suman: Yes, he (ii) _____________ or he will fall ill again. Ajay: He (iii) _____________ take grandmother's natural cures. Suman: Yes, we (iv) _____________ remember how effective they are.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Now that you have read about many spices, can you guess the spices based on the riddles given below? (1) When your tummy felt funny / I was used by your granny / I made you fit and fine / And brought back your smile. Who am I? (2) I come from under the ground / Thousands of years, I've been around. / For cooking you use me daily / From cold I can cure you surely. Who am I?

question_bank.stem: Guess the spice from the riddle: 'When your tummy felt funny / I was used by your granny / I made you fit and fine / And brought back your smile.' Who am I?
Guess the spice from the riddle: 'I come from under the ground / Thousands of years, I've been around. / For cooking you use me daily / From cold I can cure you surely.' Who am I?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Fill in the blanks to complete the riddle below. Write another riddle of your own and ask your family members to guess the spice. Riddle to complete: 'Brownish-_______ in colour, and _______ to taste, I help everyone, to _______ their weight. Who am I? Ans: Fenugreek (methi)'. Write another riddle of your own.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks to complete the riddle about fenugreek: 'Brownish-_______ in colour, and _______ to taste, I help everyone, to _______ their weight.'
Write a riddle of your own about a spice and ask your family members to guess it.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Close your eyes and learn about spices by smelling and feeling them. The teacher may also provide a few different spices to try.
